findings.message: bulk_create_items commits inside the per-item loop, so the batch is not atomic: if any insert after the first fails (constraint violation, connection error, etc.), earlier items remain persisted while later ones are lost — a partial-write that callers expecting bulk semantics cannot detect or roll back. This violates the all-or-nothing invariant typically expected of a bulk-create API and makes retries unsafe (re-running may double-insert the items that already succeeded).
